claim: Iran shot down a U.S. F-35 Lightning II stealth fighter jet, with one crew member rescued by U.S. forces and a search ongoing for the second crew member.
source: U.S. Military and Iranian Government Officials

claim: Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Abbas Aragchi announced the downing of the U.S. F-35 as a 'victory' and claimed the pilot is in Iranian custody.
President Trump warned of further U.S. strikes on Iranian bridges and power plants and stated the U.S. war with Iran could end 'very soon' or within weeks.
Iran's parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf rejected U.S. demands for surrender and warned against ground operations.
Iran maintains control of the Strait of Hormuz with an 'iron grip,' allowing limited oil tanker passage mostly of its own ships.
Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei died in February 28 U.S.-Israeli strikes, and Tehran's Assembly of Experts appointed Mojtaba Khamenei, the former Supreme Leader's son, as his successor.
Mojtaba Khamenei recently issued a rare statement indicating Iran's adversaries need to have their security removed.
Iran appointed Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr, a hardline former deputy commander of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, as the new chief of the Supreme National Security Council.
Ali Larijani, former chief of the Supreme National Security Council, was killed in an Israeli strike.
Iran told mediators it is unwilling to meet U.S. officials in Islamabad to discuss a ceasefire.
Iran's parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf stated on social media that 'no negotiations' were held with the United States.

The U.S. presented a fifteen-point peace proposal to Iran via Pakistan encompassing reopening the Strait of Hormuz, placing limits on missiles, sanctions relief, and a rollback on Iran's nuclear program.
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said Tehran was reviewing the U.S. peace proposal but insisted it did not intend to negotiate.
A senior Iranian official stated the war would only end when Tehran's conditions are met, including an end to fighting on 'all fronts' and guarantees Iran will not be attacked in the future.
Iran executed two political prisoners after their death sentences were upheld in security-related cases.
Iranian hardliners branded former Foreign Minister Mohammad Javad Zarif a 'traitor' and accused him of espionage over his call to end the war.
Iranian lawmakers urged the arrest of former Foreign Minister Mohammad Javad Zarif.
Iran's state broadcaster adopted a noticeably harsher tone toward dissent, increasingly framing domestic protests as part of a war waged by 'enemies'.
Residents reported severe shortages and soaring prices for key medicines, including insulin and blood thinners, with some insulin brands reaching seventy million rials (approximately $46.7).
Dozens of IRGC-linked money changers were arrested in the United Arab Emirates in a Dubai crackdown that struck a significant blow to Iran's sanctions-evasion network.
Dubai's crackdown on money changers exposed how heavily the Islamic Republic has depended on Dubai as an economic lifeline.
Bahrain raised concerns at the UN about Iranian threats to the Strait of Hormuz, warning that escalating Iranian aggression endangers the global economy.
The IRGC is consolidating power amid the most significant political upheaval in Iran since the 1979 revolution, driven by convergence of war, economic crisis, and a growing leadership vacuum.
Egypt and Turkey are searching for alternate solutions to mediate ceasefire talks between the U.S. and Iran.
